title: Innovative Crisp Malaysian Linguine Pasta
description: A plant-based Malaysian dish featuring linguine pasta, beurre-monte, and a blend of Asian flavors, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This meal-prep friendly recipe is cultured, crisp, and delicious.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the linguine pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, coconut milk, and chili flakes, then pour the mixture over the mushrooms and stir to combine.
Stir in the beurre-monte and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Drain the cooked linguine pasta and add it to the skillet, tossing to combine with the mushroom sauce.
Season the pasta with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
Garnish with chopped scallions or sesame seeds, if desired.
Let the pasta cool completely, then portion into individual containers for meal prep.
